featureName: BUG-026-005: Stale code/test path references in spec 026 scopes.md (gaps-to-doc round 1 close-out)

completedPhaseClaimDetails.summary: Trigger probe of specs/026-domain-extraction at HEAD 773100f1 (gaps-to-doc mapped child mode under sweep-2026-05-24-r10 round 1). Ran an inline regex extraction over scopes.md backtick-quoted code/test paths, then os.path.exists() each. 5 path-mismatch hits in 4 distinct paths: internal/db/migrations/015_domain_extraction.sql x2, internal/db/migrations_test.go x1, tests/integration/domain_extraction_test.go x2. 2 additional hits (internal/nats/domain_subjects.go, internal/api/domain_search.go) confirmed out-of-scope because their scopes.md citations carry explicit 'or use existing file' hedging parentheticals. Findings routed into the BUG-026-005 packet for one-to-one closure.

Created the BUG-026-005 6-artifact packet (bug.md, spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, report.md, uservalidation.md) for 4 distinct stale code/test path references in specs/026-domain-extraction/scopes.md surfaced by sweep-2026-05-24-r10 round 1 bubbles.gaps trigger probe (mapped child mode: gaps-to-doc). Findings classified as PATH_MISMATCH narrative drift; severity Low (zero runtime impact, no guard failure). Out-of-scope: 2 documented design-alternative paths in scopes.md at lines 363 and 886 that already carry explicit 'or use existing file' hedging parentheticals.

Applied 4 targeted text replacements to specs/026-domain-extraction/scopes.md via multi_replace_string_in_file: (1) line 58 SQL header block updated from 'internal/db/migrations/015_domain_extraction.sql' to 'internal/db/migrations/archive/015_domain_extraction.sql' with consolidation parenthetical; (2) line 134 narrative bullet updated to the same canonical form; (3) line 151 T1-06 row updated to cite 'tests/integration/db_migration_test.go::TestMigrations_ArtifactsColumns' (type changed from 'unit' to 'integration'); (4) lines 776/777 T7-05/T7-06 rows updated to cite 'tests/e2e/domain_e2e_test.go::TestE2E_DomainExtraction' (type changed from 'integration' to 'e2e'). Also appended BUG-026-005 to specs/026-domain-extraction/state.json::resolvedBugs[] and advanced lastUpdatedAt. Zero runtime code, schema, migration, NATS contract, prompt contract, ML sidecar, web template, Telegram command, or config value touched.

Re-ran the inline path-drift probe on scopes.md: returns only the 2 documented out-of-scope design-alternative paths (internal/api/domain_search.go and internal/nats/domain_subjects.go, both with hedging parentheticals). All 4 in-scope stale references are gone. JSON validity of parent 026 state.json verified with python3 -c 'import json; json.load(...)'.

All three framework guards on parent 026 continue to PASS: state-transition-guard.sh (TRANSITION PERMITTED with same 2 pre-existing advisory warnings), artifact-lint.sh (PASS), traceability-guard.sh (PASS). All three guards on the BUG-026-005 packet also PASS. Parent 026 status stays 'done' end-to-end; this bug's two scopes both Done with checked DoD evidence.

git diff --name-only HEAD lists only allowed paths: BUG-026-005 packet (6 files + state.json) plus specs/026-domain-extraction/scopes.md plus specs/026-domain-extraction/state.json. No out-of-scope edits. PII redaction verified: zero /home/<user>/ paths in evidence blocks. Commit prefix 'bubbles(026/bug-026-005): ...' satisfies Check 17 structured commit gate for spec 026.

Completion Statement added to report.md. Parent 026 state.json::resolvedBugs[] gains BUG-026-005 entry with resolvedAt and resolution paragraph. state.json status -> resolved, certification.status -> resolved.

Confirmed zero runtime regression. BUG packet edits only specs/026-domain-extraction/{scopes.md, state.json} and the BUG-026-005 packet folder. tests/e2e/domain_e2e_test.go::TestE2E_DomainExtraction last GREEN at HEAD 1587df4d in sweep-2026-05-23-r30 rounds 10/19; runtime path unchanged so protected scenarios remain green by construction.

No structural simplification opportunity. The 4 scopes.md edits are the minimum mechanical text replacements required to fix the 4 PATH_MISMATCH findings; nothing speculative or duplicative was added.

Behavior stability confirmed. Zero runtime path, zero compiled code, zero migration, zero schema, zero NATS contract change. Parent spec 026 retained status=done end-to-end; no flake risk introduced; no test reordering or fixture mutation occurred.

Security posture preserved. Zero secret, credential, token, or auth path touched. gitleaks pre-commit hook ran on the staging set; all PII (linux-home-username-leak) was redacted to relative or generic forms in every evidence block before staging.

Chaos probe N/A for this narrative-only fix — zero runtime code, schema, migration, NATS contract, prompt contract, ML sidecar, web template, Telegram command, or config value is changed. There is no behavioral surface to perturb. The trigger-mapped child mode (gaps-to-doc) does not require chaos probing for spec-artifact reconciliation. Recorded as executed-N/A to satisfy gaps-to-doc phase contract (G022) and to make the no-perturbation decision auditable.
